introSection: Alps, originally known as Mountain Scenic Railway, was a wooden roller coaster previously located at Six Gun Territory (originally known as Willow Grove Park) in Willow Grove, Pennsylvania, USA. The roller coaster traveled through and around a faux mountain.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
The ride opened in 1905 as Mountain Scenic Railway. It was renamed in 1934 to Alps.

The ride was re-worked by Philadelphia Toboggan Company and Herbert P. Schmeck during the 1939-1940 off-season.

The ride closed with the park in 1975.

After standing but not operating since the park's closure, the ride was demolished in 1980.

fullText:
Alps, originally known as Mountain Scenic Railway, was a wooden roller coaster previously located at Six Gun Territory (originally known as Willow Grove Park) in Willow Grove, Pennsylvania, USA. The roller coaster traveled through and around a faux mountain.

The ride opened in 1905 as Mountain Scenic Railway. It was renamed in 1934 to Alps.

The ride was re-worked by Philadelphia Toboggan Company and Herbert P. Schmeck during the 1939-1940 off-season.

The ride closed with the park in 1975.

After standing but not operating since the park's closure, the ride was demolished in 1980.
